2605.05686.title: Attractor Geometry of Transformer Memory: From Conflict Arbitration to Confident Hallucination
2605.05686.paper_summary: Language models draw on two knowledge sources: facts baked into weights (parametric memory, PM) and information in context (working memory, WM). We study two mechanistically distinct failure modes--conflict, when PM and WM disagree and interfere; and hallucination, when the queried fact was never learned. Both produce confident output regardless, making output-based monitoring blind by design. We show both failures share a unified geometric account. In the hidden-state space of autoregressive generation, learned facts form attractor basins. Conflict is basin competition: WM disrupts convergence to the correct basin without raising output entropy. Hallucination is basin absence: the hidden state drifts freely when no memorized basin exists. The frozen LM head, designed for next-token prediction, cannot distinguish these cases and fires confidently either way. We verify this account in a controlled synthetic task--entity identifiers mapped to unique codes with PM installed via LoRA adapters--where ground truth is exact and component roles can be causally isolated through targeted adapter placement. Geometric margin--the hidden state's distance to the nearest memorized basin--reads this geometry directly and separates correct recall from hallucination far more cleanly than output entropy, with zero false refusals where entropy-based detection cannot avoid rejecting the vast majority of correct outputs. The separation holds on natural-language factual queries from the pretrained model with no adaptation, confirming attractor geometry is structural rather than a fine-tuning artifact. The fraction of confident hallucinations follows a scaling law $C = \exp(-c/\barΔ)$, growing with scale even as overall error rates fall. Hidden states reliably encode epistemic state; the frozen output head systematically erases it--and this erasure worsens with scale.
2605.05686.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型依賴兩種知識來源：內嵌於權重中的事實（參數記憶，PM）和上下文中的信息（工作記憶，WM）。我們研究兩種機制上不同的失效模式——衝突，當PM和WM不一致並相互干擾時；以及幻覺，當被查詢的事實從未學習過時。無論如何，這兩者都會產生自信的輸出，這使得基於輸出的監控在設計上是盲目的。我們展示這兩種失效共享一個統一的幾何解釋。在自回歸生成的隱藏狀態空間中，學習到的事實形成吸引子盆地。衝突是盆地競爭：WM在不提高輸出熵的情況下破壞了對正確盆地的收斂。幻覺是盆地缺失：當不存在任何記憶盆地時，隱藏狀態自由漂移。為下一個標記預測設計的凍結LM頭無法區分這些情況，並且無論如何都會自信地觸發。我們在一個受控的合成任務中驗證了這一解釋——實體標識符映射到唯一代碼，並通過LoRA適配器安裝PM——其中真實情況是精確的，並且組件角色可以通過有針對性的適配器放置進行因果隔離。幾何邊際——隱藏狀態到最近記憶盆地的距離——直接讀取這一幾何，並比輸出熵更清晰地區分正確回憶和幻覺，且在熵基檢測無法避免拒絕絕大多數正確輸出的情況下，零假拒絕。這種區分在未經調整的預訓練模型的自然語言事實查詢中保持不變，確認吸引子幾何是結構性的，而非微調的產物。自信幻覺的比例遵循一個縮放法則 $C = \exp(-c/\barΔ)$，隨著規模增長，即使整體錯誤率下降。隱藏狀態可靠地編碼認知狀態；凍結的輸出頭系統性地抹去了它——而這種抹去隨著規模的增長而惡化。
2605.05686.author: Qiyao Liang et.al.
2605.05686.authors: Qiyao Liang, Risto Miikkulainen, Ila Fiete
